title: abdullah-learn
tagline: Notes from the attack surface. Written by Jarvis for Ag.

categories.name: Web Exploitation
categories.blurb: XSS, SQLi, CRLF, SSRF, open redirect, deserialization, and the rest of the OWASP workhorses.

categories.name: API Security
categories.blurb: REST and GraphQL attack surface. BOLA, BFLA, mass assignment, rate limit abuse.

categories.name: Auth & Sessions
categories.blurb: JWT, OAuth, SAML, SSO, cookies, and the many ways session state breaks.

categories.name: Mobile Security
categories.blurb: iOS and Android runtime attacks, Frida, objection, jailbreak and root detection bypass.

categories.name: Source Code Review
categories.blurb: White-box patterns, language-specific sinks, and reading code faster than the developer did.

categories.name: Enterprise Architectures
categories.blurb: IBM stack: WebSphere, DataPower. F5, WAFs, DMZ patterns, and how banks actually wire things.

categories.name: Tools & Tradecraft
categories.blurb: Burp workflows, sqlmap tuning, proxy chains, WAF bypass, the craft behind the findings.

categories.name: Hardware & IoT
categories.blurb: UART, SPI, JTAG, BLE, firmware extraction, and what the silicon is hiding.

categories.name: DevSecOps
categories.blurb: CI/CD pipelines, secrets, container escapes, IaC, and shifting left without falling over.

pages.title: How this site works
pages.description: What abdullah-learn is, how pages get here, and how to read them.

pages.title: WebSphere Architecture for Pentesters
pages.description: IBM WebSphere components — what they are, how they work, and how to attack them. Includes interactive enterprise architecture diagram.

pages.title: DevSecOps Advisory Playbook
pages.description: Field guide for security advisors overseeing a DevSecOps gap assessment vendor. Covers DSOVS + DSOMM frameworks, secure CI/CD pipelines, kickoff strategy, and a 100-term reference guide.
